review:
Came a few days ago for a lease, wasn't sure of size needed, so I guessed, three times! Finally got it right, but hey, the store didn't bat a eye lash when I returned the ones that didn't work, they just asked if I needed help picking out a replacement. 

Since my cat has been loosing weight, I could not get the size down, so after my attempts, finally got the small dog size and sure enough it worked. Now to get the cat used to it before we need it. 

This store has everything you could need. They is even a new section by Martha Stewart, everything for you little pet. But her stuffs pricey, a lease from here collection, $19.99, boy that's steep! 

The store is clean, neatly kept, well organized and they have grooming services. The employees were friendly and helpful, they looked like they enjoyed their jobs, and I would make this a regular place.
key_point: The staff were friendly and helpful with returns and recommendations, and the store is clean and well-organized.
business_response: Thank you so much for your kind words and for sharing your experience! We're glad our team could help you find the right size and that you enjoyed our store's selection and atmosphere. We look forward to welcoming you and your cat back soon!
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's main points about staff helpfulness and store cleanliness, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and encourages future visits.

review:
I chose the 4 Queens for my visit to Las Vegas for several reasons, and I'll admit a good lot of those involved the low price of the room. (Other reasons include the proximity to the Neon Boneyard, the historic old-Vegas reputation of the hotel and the ratings on Trip Advisor.)

However, the quality of my room was far below what I expected, or even dreamed was possible.

I have very minimal requirements for hotel rooms; generally I only want them to be clean (not spotless, clean) and relatively safe. Non-smoking is also on the list.

My room at the 4 Queens was the filthiest hotel room I've ever stayed in.

There was hair (of the nether-region variety) in the bathtub. In fact, I don't think the shower or bathtub were touched by house cleaning before or during my stay. I don't like to make a fuss so wiped-everything down myself, and was too exhausted to switch rooms on top of that.

Every surface in the room felt greasy. I'm going to say it was covered in some Pledge-like substance (humor me) to make myself feel better.

There was an empty packet of Lucky Strike cigarettes behind the one side of the drapes and a lighter on the other when I arrived, and spotted quite easily. This was touted to be a non-smoking room. I left the empty cigarette pack and lighter for the duration of my stay to see of it would be eventually picked-up by the housekeeping staff. 

Nope; no such luck.

Additionally, there were cigarette burns in the bedding (which was made of some, scratchy, ugly cheap fabric). Two of the three lamps in the room did not work. At all. The remote did not work. The air conditioner was very, very loud.

I didn't realized that the Academy of Country Music awards we're being held in the city the weekend I stayed, and on the 15th floor, I could hear the awful cover bands from Fremont Street quite clearly. Granted, I should have realized the ACMs were the weekend of my stay, but the noise was a bit ridiculous.
Oh, and the folks staying in the room next door smoked weed every night of my stay. I dare say I could very well have gotten a contact-high the smell was so strong.

Luckily, I take Ambien to sleep, and am dead to the world at night. Even with the Ambien, the world's rattliest, loudest air conditioner, still woke me up several times each night. This is in addition to being woken by the amazingly loud sex the reefer users next door were having. Otherwise there is absolutely no way I would have gotten any rest in this hellhole.

Update! I initially wrote this review with one night of my stay remaining. Guess what? It got worse! The country band playing on the street on Saturday night was somehow (defying the laws of sound) louder, and played later into the evening. Once my Reefer Madness neighbors returned to their room at near 4am, they rented several porn movies and acted out scenes from them. This went on until about 8 am. I pounded on the wall twice, called the front desk, and nothing was done! I didn't sleep a wink despite the Ambien.

I finally headed to the airport around 9 am, five hours early for my flight, because the hotel was just that damn bad, and I couldn't stand the filthiness and sleaze any longer.

Absolutely the worst hotel I've ever stayed in. The only thing that could have made it worse are bed bugs or a fire possibly breaking-out.
key_point: The room was extremely dirty, with evidence of poor housekeeping and smoking in a non-smoking room.
business_response: We are truly sorry to hear about the cleanliness issues you experienced during your stay. This is not the standard we strive to uphold, and your feedback will be shared with our housekeeping team for immediate improvement. Please contact us directly so we can address your concerns and work to make this right.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the cleanliness issues but incorrectly mentions smoking, which was not explicitly stated as a problem; the business_response is polite, relevant, and offers a path for resolution.

review:
I went here on the day of a wedding (I'm from out of town) for a last minute pedicure. I didn't really know where else to go, so I just stopped here on a whim (it was raining and this was the closest place that seemed to do nails). 

The Salon Nuvo nail person is FANTASTIC (and wonderful to chat with). It's two full weeks later and it looks like my pedicure is brand new (and before you ask, I actually used the exact same brand/color nail polish as my prior crap pedicure). They don't have the cool massage chairs and they put you in a weird little intimate back room, HOWEVER I would totally give that up for a beautiful pedicure that lasts this long.  

Thought it was worth a review.
key_point: The pedicure lasted for weeks and the nail technician was fantastic, despite the lack of massage chairs.
business_response: Thank you so much for your wonderful review! We're thrilled to hear that you loved your pedicure and enjoyed your experience with our nail technician. We appreciate your feedback about our setup and are glad the quality made your visit worthwhile—hope to see you again next time you're in town!
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's main points, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and professionally addresses the feedback.

review: Isn't it strange how the little things can sour your experience? I had a good time at Paris, the dealers were friendly, the staff was nice, the rooms were clean until I was checking out and the guy at the taxi line started yelling that my car was parked to far over. And he kept screaming and gesturing until I moved my car over until it was blocking traffic, which I was trying to avoid in the first place. I usually stay here but I won't be anymore. He was the guy on duty 2/17 at 12pm.
key_point: A staff member at the taxi line was rude and yelled at the customer during checkout, souring an otherwise positive experience.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback and bringing this incident to our attention. We sincerely apologize for the behavior you experienced at the taxi line and will address this with our team to ensure it does not happen again. We hope you'll consider giving us another chance in the future, as we value your loyalty and want every aspect of your stay to be enjoyable.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main issue, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's concern professionally.

review: Visit here several times a year. The food is always fresh and well stocked. They have sushi, and several seafood items for their dinner buffet. The staff is always efficient as well.
key_point: The food is always fresh and well stocked, and the staff is efficient.
business_response: Thank you so much for your continued support and kind words! We're delighted to hear you enjoy our fresh food selection and attentive staff. We look forward to welcoming you back again soon.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and professionally acknowledges the customer's feedback.

review: I bought a voucher for this place from one of the deal sites. They accidentally crossed of my name on their list showing that my voucher was already used when it hadn't been. They then proceed to tell me that their verification method was fool proof (how can it be when nothing is scanned and everything is manually checked off by hand?) Then they tried to prove that the voucher I brought in was a photocopy reproduction (it wasn't, it was printer from a printer). All in all, a terrible experience, tried to make me out to be a thief. Why would I even go through all that trouble and reproduce a very \"real looking\" fake voucher to save $10?!? At the end they realized their mistake. By then I had already 25 mins of my time and my order had already gotten cold. There were no apologies from the restaurant. The food was also very salty and the bubble tea was very chalky. They use powered coffeemate to cream their teas which has a huge taste difference from actual condensed or fresh milk. With all the Chinese food options in that area, I know I will never go back to this place.
key_point: The customer had a negative experience with voucher verification, felt accused of dishonesty, received no apology, and was dissatisfied with the food and drinks.
business_response: We sincerely apologize for the confusion and inconvenience you experienced with our voucher process and for any discomfort caused by our staff's actions. We also regret that our food and drinks did not meet your expectations. Your feedback is very important to us, and we will use it to improve both our service and our menu offerings.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the main issues in the review, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and addresses both the service and food concerns professionally.

review:
Like others, I'm writing a post-mortem tribute to one of my favorite restaurants in the world.

My husband and I first went to Rosemary's on our honeymoon in 2001. We've been there on every trip to LV since, and we also went to the second restaurant they had at the Rio for a while. Every  visit was a delight.

Michael and Wendy, as someone who recently closed a restaurant (and is in the process of selling a second one) my heart goes out to you.  Know that you are missed, and that  I (and many many others, I'm sure) am monitoring the internet to see what you have in store for us next.

~~A fan from Texas
key_point: The reviewer expresses heartfelt appreciation and fond memories of the restaurant, lamenting its closure.
business_response: Thank you so much for your kind words and for being such a loyal guest over the years. Your support and memories mean the world to us during this transition. We hope to share exciting news with you and our wonderful community in the future—please stay in touch!
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's sentiment and content, and the business_response is warm, relevant, and encourages ongoing engagement.

review: My mom and I just ate lunch there and I would never go back. We both live in the area and were excited to try it. The service was terrible. It took us forever to get our food and when it did come my pad thai was ok not great and my moms veggie torta was also mediocre. The main reason I will never go back is when we ordered desert our server verbally told us the menu and we ordered the cheesecake. When it came it was covered in peanuts after I specifically told the server I was allergic when I ordered the pad thai peanut free. I'm leaving on a flight and didn't have time for them to make me something else. So overall mediocre food and terrible service.
key_point: The reviewer experienced poor service, mediocre food, and a serious allergy oversight with their dessert.
business_response: We are truly sorry to hear about your disappointing experience and especially concerned about the allergy issue with your dessert. Your feedback is very important to us, and we will address these concerns with our team to ensure this does not happen again. Thank you for bringing this to our attention, and we hope you will consider giving us another chance in the future.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main issues, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the allergy concern professionally.

review: Only reason they get two stars is because their pies are great. Terrible service and don't have enough people working so it takes half an hour to get anyone to even acknowledge that you are waiting.
key_point: The pies are great, but the service is very slow and understaffed.
business_response: Thank you for your honest feedback and for complimenting our pies. We sincerely apologize for the slow service you experienced and understand how frustrating it can be to wait. We're working to improve our staffing and service times, and we hope you'll give us another chance in the future.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the concerns raised.

review: The place has a nice atmosphere, and they've made an effort to make this basement location feel more lively, colourful and 'south americanish'. The service is really nice and friendly as well, and I'm pretty sure most of the waiters speak spanish which is a good sign when you're eating in a south american place!\nSo why only 3 stars? I love mexican/southern food so maybe I'm a slightly harsh critic, but I just wasn't really taken with the menu. \nEverything we ordered was a bit too overflowing with cheese and creme fraiche so the vibrant flavours were drowned out. The menu isn't really expensive, \u00a315 for 2 courses, but considering the fierce competition with set menus in Edina, it's probably a touch high for such simple food (plain rice, beans, chicken, flour tortillas and side salad)
key_point: The atmosphere and service are great, but the food was too heavy on cheese and creme fraiche, and the menu felt a bit overpriced for its simplicity.
business_response: Thank you for your thoughtful feedback and for highlighting our atmosphere and friendly service. We appreciate your honest comments about the menu and will share your suggestions with our kitchen team to help improve the balance of flavors. We hope you'll give us another try in the future as we continue to refine our offerings.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's praise and criticisms, and the business response is polite, relevant, and shows willingness to improve.

review: Very slow -- even without a lot of customers -- and very disorganized. You can't pay with the Starbucks app -- they have to take your phone and key in the number by hand!
key_point: Service was slow and disorganized, and mobile payment was inconvenient.
business_response: Thank you for your feedback and for bringing these concerns to our attention. We apologize for the slow service and the inconvenience with our payment process. We're working on improving our efficiency and updating our payment systems to better serve our customers.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and addresses both issues raised.

review: Food is ok but for the love of Pete lose the  iPad check pay system! We want paper checks!!!  After having lunch with friends we were shown our individual bills on an iPad and asked to finger sign. Lunch with one entree and no drink was over $22 each with tax and tip. Really?  No detail of our bills and no paper receipts. Bad!!!
key_point: The customer is frustrated with the iPad payment system and lack of paper receipts.
business_response: Thank you for your feedback and for dining with us. We apologize for any inconvenience caused by our digital payment system and understand your preference for paper receipts. We will review our procedures to ensure a smoother experience and appreciate your input as we strive to improve.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the main complaint about the iPad payment system and lack of paper receipts, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's concerns professionally.

review: I was honestly just shocked to find Gluten Free pizza`so close to home!  While I can't vouch for the reguar pizza, I would recommend the GF pizza without a doubt.  The only downside is that it takes a much longer time to prepare, but I don't think that's the fault of Fuel.  The people are always friendly and more than happy to keep us entertained while we wait for our pizza!
key_point: The reviewer highly recommends the gluten-free pizza and appreciates the friendly staff, though notes it takes longer to prepare.
business_response: Thank you so much for your kind words and recommendation of our gluten-free pizza! We appreciate your patience with the longer preparation time and are glad our team could make your experience enjoyable. We look forward to welcoming you back soon!
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and professionally useful.

review:
DO NOT GET KEYS MADE HERE!

I am so annoyed with this Ace. I moved into a new place and needed to get keys made. The cashier paged someone and after waiting about 15 minutes I headed toward the exit, then he finally arrived. I had 5 keys made, 2 didn't fit.  Went back to have the 2 fixed, 1 still didn't work. Ugh!

Fast forward a year later and keep in mind my deteriorating memory, I once again had to get keys made and returned to this location. I got 5 made, 2 didn't work (sound familiar). When I went  back to get the keys fixed the woman who helped me told me it is not usually the fault of the people cutting the keys, but the calibration of the machine. She assured me the machine was just calibrated so we should have no issues. 

And guess what? One of the keys STILL doesn't fit.
key_point: Multiple visits resulted in keys that did not work, with ongoing issues despite assurances about machine calibration.
business_response: We're truly sorry for the repeated inconvenience you've experienced with our key cutting service. Your feedback is important, and we will review our machine calibration process and staff training to ensure better results in the future. Please contact us directly so we can make this right for you.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the repeated issues described in the review, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and offers a constructive next step.

review: Honest, good group of people.  They don't try to sell you tires every time you come in.  They have fixed my tires twice, rotated twice and have not charged me.  Chad was very helpful and pleasant.  This is the best Discount Tire in the area.  4.5 only because they didn't tighten my hub cover the last time I was there and almost lost it.
key_point: Great service and honesty, but a hub cover was not tightened properly during the last visit.
business_response: Thank you for your kind words and for highlighting Chad's helpfulness. We apologize for the oversight with your hub cover and appreciate you bringing it to our attention. Your feedback helps us improve, and we hope to serve you even better next time.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes both the positive and negative aspects of the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's feedback professionally.

review:
This Panera (Gosh, they popped up everywhere) is standard Panera I'd say. Smaller patio on the sidewalk and not the most inviting of patios in the small strip of a larger shopping area with Lowe's and Walmart . I usually find someone will come and smoke near you if you sit outside here for a little while (Must resist the urge to slap the person).
Usually clean but have found the Internets here a little slower than other Panera's. 
Bagel's are pretty good but get chewier as the day progresses and i don't usually make it to a Panera until the evening.  Find the food to be a little pricer for the same fare as Jason's Deli or McCalister's but always pretty fresh and good. 
Love the Chicken Noodle Soup though, my favorite!!
key_point: The reviewer enjoys the fresh food, especially the Chicken Noodle Soup, but notes the patio is less inviting, Wi-Fi can be slow, and prices are a bit higher than competitors.
business_response: Thank you for your thoughtful feedback and for being a fan of our Chicken Noodle Soup! We appreciate your comments about the patio, Wi-Fi, and pricing, and will share them with our team as we strive to improve. We hope to continue serving you fresh meals and a welcoming experience on every visit.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main positives and negatives, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the feedback professionally.

review: 4 stars for being able to get a tasty and good-sized portion of spicy Malaysian Chicken Fried Rice, late afternoon without leaving Bellagio. Soaked up some of the day's excess alcohol wonderfully.\n\nA star off for having to pay Bellagio prices for the above.
key_point: The customer enjoyed the tasty and generous portion of Malaysian Chicken Fried Rice but felt the prices were high.
business_response: Thank you for your feedback and for choosing us for your late afternoon meal. We're glad you enjoyed the Malaysian Chicken Fried Rice and appreciate your comments about our pricing. We strive to offer quality and value, and your input helps us improve the experience for all our guests.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and professionally useful.

review: SHC Crossfit is Fantastic... It has changed my life, seriously, I love it.  I started crossfit at another box that was twice the cost of this box.  SHC Crossfit is not only less expensive it also has far better coaching than my 1st box!  Come check out SHC Crossfit... you won't be disappointed.
key_point: The reviewer praises SHC Crossfit for its life-changing impact, excellent coaching, and affordable pricing.
business_response: Thank you so much for your wonderful review! We're thrilled to hear that you've had such a positive experience with our coaching and pricing. We appreciate your recommendation and look forward to supporting you on your fitness journey.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's main points, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and professionally acknowledges the feedback.

review: I stayed at the Palazzo but accidentally checked in at the Venetian. The lady that checked me in was very rude and talk down to me. Every question I asked she acted like I was burdening her. She then gave me directions to the Venetian side and we carried our luggage all the way to the 18th floor not realizing we had to go to the 18th floor of the Palazzo. She checked me in, she should have directed metro the palazzo. The Palazzo hotel was great.
key_point: The front desk staff at the Venetian was rude and gave incorrect directions, causing inconvenience.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We sincerely apologize for the confusion and the way you were treated at check-in, as this is not the standard we strive for. We're glad you enjoyed your stay at the Palazzo, and we will address your feedback with our team to ensure better service in the future.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main issues, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's concerns professionally.

review:
Eh.  For the big Irish pub on the square, this is just average.  I like the ambiance.  It is a place to sit and have a pint while chatting with friends.  It never gets too loud.  Upstairs, the couches are fun, almost Alice in Wonderland inspired.  

Prices are a bit high, but if you happen to luck out for happy hour then you are golden.  They have happy hour everyday from 10pm to close.  It is a nice place to end your day and get a deal. 

I have had their food a couple of time.  It is ok.  I am just not overly excited about this place.  They always card. ALWAYS.  Kind of annoying because I am obviously over 21 and there creates this HUGE congestion at the door.  

I do not hate this place but I do not love it.
key_point: The ambiance is enjoyable and happy hour is a good deal, but the food is average and the strict carding policy causes congestion at the door.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your honest feedback. We're glad you enjoy our ambiance and happy hour specials, and we appreciate your comments about our food and entrance process. We'll take your suggestions into consideration to improve the experience for all our guests.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and shows willingness to improve.

review:
For a place that just opens pre-made packages and plops them on a salad-bar, I would think that this effort might not have been to challenging. Perhaps I had my hopes up too high.

I went here for a quick bite as I had business in the neighborhood. I must say, I would have done better anywhere else. The salad selection was less than fresh to say the least. A person kept barging out of the back to refill things, and was super surley. Just get in her way and get an elbow.

After the salad selection are random canned things. I guess those were ok. I am not sure.

They also have a "hot bar". This is where things get really depressing. They had a kind-of taco set-up thing going with what appeared to be ground beef. Except it wasn't! It was straight-up textured vegetable protein! Who even does that? I got a baked sweet potato, and that thing had been sitting under a heat lamp for what seemed weeks and was inedible. The soups were just blah. Who serves french onion soup without the topping? The pizzas they had on the platter were less than what you would expect from the cheapest of frozen pizzas.

Just all in all horrible. I left having drank and unsweetened ice tea and left a plate from the all-you-can-eat salad bar totally full.

What a waste of time and money. This place is gross.
key_point: The reviewer was disappointed by the freshness and quality of the food, as well as the service and overall experience.
business_response: We're very sorry to hear about your disappointing experience and appreciate your detailed feedback. We strive to provide fresh food and friendly service, and clearly fell short during your visit. If you'd be willing to reach out to us directly, we'd like to learn more and see how we can make things right.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main complaints about food quality and service, and the business response is polite, relevant, and invites further contact to resolve the issue.

review:
I would give this place 2.5 stars.
There was nothing really terrible about kofusion but there wasn't anything that great about it either!
Atmosphere- fine. A little sparkly for my taste but whatever. It was busy, otherwise sitting outside on the patio would have been much nicer.
Service- I know it was busy but the service was a mess. The food came out really fast but none of us had chopsticks, or soy sauce, or ginger, or wasabi, and they were about to just leave us! Not to mention trying to get a drink!
Food- pretty bland and mushy with a couple good standouts on my sampler plate. I swear, one of the bad ones must have been the bologna of the sea because it looked/tasted like a hunk of spam stuffed into a roll form topped with some sauce. I spit that one out. 
There were a couple good pieces that after I try every other sushi restaurant in C-U I might come back for.
key_point: Service was disorganized and the food was mostly bland, with only a few standout items.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We're sorry to hear that your experience with our service and some of our dishes did not meet your expectations. We appreciate your honest comments and will use them to improve both our service and menu offerings.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main criticisms, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's concerns professionally.

review:
I'm on a quest to find a phoenix and dragon similar to my favorite Chinese place in Indy. This is the closet I've come, much to my disappointment. This restaurant keeps getting into my apartment building, despite the security doors. Today they just stuck them in the elevator. Which I thought was pretty neat compared to their normal door spamiming. Then I went down to the first floor and realized they also just scattered them all over the floor.

In my phoenix and dragon I had five "jumbo" shrimps. Which are really just average sized shrimp. The only thing I can think of is that they consider the mini shrimp some Chinese places have as normal. I guess compared to those the shrimp is jumbo. The "spicy chicken" is just general tso's. I got medium spice which was barely spicy at all. Most of my dish ended up being peas, carrots, and onions in a weird "spicy" sauce that wasn't very good.

I also got the rangoons. Some one else mentioned in this that they don't really have much crab. Which is an understatement. There's maybe a flick of crab in each. They're still fairly good, just not crab.

I suppose the worst part was delivery time. The confirmation email doesn't come with a estimated delivery. Whatever, my boyfriend and I expect 40 minutes like normal. 40 minute mark rolls around and we haven't gotten a call. Since our buzzer system isn't set up we were worried that they didn't see the note to call and were buzzing us. The person who answers says it's in the car and close. 20 minutes later we're still waiting. My boyfriend calls back and goes to give his info. The lady says, "Oh, you just called. It's almost there," and that was it. Uhm. Ok. A little less than 10 minutes later and it arrives. The driver apologized and It was still hot. So there's that.

tl;dr small portions, not jumbo shrimp, not spicy, not very good, long wait, unfriendly staff on the phones, friendly drivers, and they litter my apartment building a lot.
key_point: The reviewer was disappointed with the food quality, misleading portion sizes, lack of spice, and poor delivery practices.
business_response: Thank you for your detailed feedback. We're sorry to hear that your experience with our food and delivery did not meet your expectations. We appreciate your comments and will use them to improve our menu descriptions, food quality, and delivery process to better serve our customers in the future.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main complaints, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the issues raised.

review: Really good Chinese food.  The duck and pork noodle soup is awesome.  Portions are not that generous so be advised but worth it for Chinese hotel strip food.
key_point: Delicious food, especially the duck and pork noodle soup, but portions could be larger.
business_response: Thank you for your kind words about our duck and pork noodle soup! We appreciate your feedback regarding portion sizes and will take it into consideration. We're glad you enjoyed your meal and hope to see you again soon.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's praise and portion comment, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and addresses the feedback constructively.

review: 777.... that's it, you don't wanna try anything else, best steak ever. Never disappoints you..my favorite late night dinning place
key_point: The customer loves the steak and consistently enjoys late-night dining at the restaurant.
business_response: Thank you so much for your kind words and for making us your favorite late-night dining spot! We're thrilled to hear you enjoy our steak and appreciate your continued support. We look forward to welcoming you back again soon!
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and professionally acknowledges the customer's feedback.

review: Wow. This was a waste of time. It seemed that they wanted to teach about a couple of habitats and be a zoo but they didn't have enough funds to realize the objective. There are a few animals and some displays about habitats, but we found this to be really disappointing.
key_point: The customer was disappointed by the limited number of animals and displays, feeling the experience did not meet expectations.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback. We're sorry to hear that your visit did not meet your expectations, and we appreciate your comments about our exhibits. We are always working to improve and hope you'll consider visiting us again in the future as we continue to grow.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main complaint, and the business response is polite, relevant, and encourages future engagement.

review: Meh... It was just ok. The food looked better than it tasted :-/  I had the BBQ chicken, greens, and mac & cheese. I would give it another try, but order other items from the menu. \n\nAside from that, the service and atmosphere were great!
key_point: The food was disappointing compared to its appearance, but the service and atmosphere were great.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback and for giving us a try. We're glad you enjoyed our service and atmosphere, and we appreciate your honesty about the food. We hope you'll visit again and try other menu items—we're always striving to improve and provide a better experience.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and encourages the customer to return.

review:
Complete, utter, crap. Ignored by servers for hours after we sat down and it was not busy there.

Had to ask for everything. Forks? Yup we didn't get those. Water? Had to constantly ask for refills by flagging down any passing staff.

You may think the food is really good only if you haven't actually traveled outside of the fast food box.

I thought it was mediocre at best, but combine that with the horrible staff and the only experience you will have here is boredom.
key_point: The reviewer was ignored by staff and had to repeatedly ask for basic service, resulting in a poor overall experience.
business_response: We're truly sorry to hear about your disappointing experience with our service and staff. This is not the standard we strive for, and we appreciate your feedback so we can address these issues with our team. If you'd be willing to share more details, please contact us directly so we can make things right.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the main complaints from the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and invites further contact to resolve the issue.

review:
I want to start off by saying I usually hate fish and chips but agreed to try this place out with my friend as per our usual yelp event. I instantly loved it. the restaurant itself is very casual laid back feel, we ate outside however. The service was good as well. 

I ordered the salmon fish and chips in  a Guinness batter and my friend ordered the cod. We swapped one each so we can try both. I personally enjoyed the salmon more but they were both delicious. We also both split the popcorn shrimp which were great. 

A great fish and chips joint with quality ingredients thumbs up!

review:
Conveniently located inside of Caesars Palace... However, they're not a part of the Total Rewards program that Caesars is a part of. 

Why would this matter? Well, cause you can't claim your free birthday drink here! 

While not my personal favorite, it's not a bad bar to try...

review: Food was creative, flavored were good. Appetizer portions reasonable for the price and quality. Main courses however is where they fall short. Portions were far too small for the price, about $6-10 more than should be depending on what you order. Bread was a but dry for brioche but a nice light bread to have with the meal. Wine selection is very good and varied. Coming from a food town like Portland, this restaurant is nothing special but is a great addition to the chandler/sun lakes area. I would not return for dinner but would for apps and a glass of wine. They were not very busy for a Friday night either so I suspect many others feel it is overpriced for dinner, too. Would re-evaluate that for longevity.\nNice atmosphere too despite odd strip mall style location.
key_point: Main course portions were too small and overpriced, but appetizers, wine, and atmosphere were good.
business_response: Thank you for your detailed feedback and for highlighting our appetizers, wine selection, and atmosphere. We appreciate your honest comments about our main course portions and pricing, and will take them into consideration as we continue to improve. We hope to welcome you back soon for another enjoyable experience with our appetizers and wine.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses both positive and negative feedback professionally.

review: Decided to take the family out to a nice brunch, so we decided to go here. We heard good reviews so we decided to give it a try. The breakdown: Lakeview was great. The service was lagging. Constantly had to remind our server to refill waters. (Even when his section was empty). Appetizers took 30min for salmon tartare and French onion soup. By the time we got our main course we were full on bread and patience. First time here = bad experience. Food overall was ok. There's no definite wow factor.
key_point: Service was slow and inattentive, and the food was just average.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us. We're very sorry to hear that your first visit did not meet your expectations, especially regarding the service and wait times. We appreciate your comments and will use them to improve our training and guest experience.

review:
boston's. dirty, dirty, dirty boston's. 

the beer is flat, the decor is deteriorating before your eyes (literally saw a ceiling tile fall on someone.), and the offtrack betting is amazing and makes you feel even dirtier!

but this is a dive bar and you should have no standards when you walk in here. if you have standards all the time you should probably go hit up some glitzy, clean chain bar that serves those drinks with little umbrellas in them. this is dirty boston's.

they serve minipitchers during happy hour for around 3.50 depending on if you like domestic or you want to get fancy with an imported beer. service has always been friendly and actually pretty prompt. bathrooms are scary, but c'mon, didn't i tell you this was a dive bar? don't go looking for ambience, just come here if you'd like to get really drunk really fast. priorities.
key_point: The bar is very dirty and rundown, with flat beer and deteriorating decor, but the service is friendly and prompt.
business_response: Thank you for your honest feedback and for highlighting our friendly service. We understand that our dive bar atmosphere isn't for everyone, but we take your comments about cleanliness and maintenance seriously and will address these concerns. We appreciate your visit and hope to provide a better experience in the future.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main criticisms and praise, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the concerns professionally.

The Well Spa was very relaxing.  I had a pedicure and gel manicure from Dan.  He did a wonderful job, was very professional as well as personable.  If I lived here, I would utilize Dan's services on a regular basis.

The waitress never came by the table after the food was brought out so we couldn't inform her that our burgers were cold and our orders were wrong. Our drinks hadn't been refilled at all. You'd think at $8 a beer they'd at least offer to get you another one. I know they're new, but at $15 a burger, they should have great service to compensate.

Service was awesome place was cool worth every penny. If you are havin hard time thinking prices are high it's def worth it. Will go back my next Vegas trip!

review: I've been here 3 times.  Twice thru the drive thru and the last time I dined in.  So glad I did.  One of the cooks (stawky white guy) wasn't wearing gloves.  Then he went to the restroom and bumped my chair and elbow in passing without a word. I went to use the bathroom and guess what, no paper towels.  So I used the blower which made a lot of noise.  That told me the cook failed to wash his hands.  When I walked out of the bathroom,  he had a hand full of bacon.  My friend had the bacon burger lol.  Oh,  did I mention that when he bent over all you saw was crack.  I'll go back but only if he's not there. The burgers and fries are great.

We sincerely apologize for your experience regarding our staff's hygiene and professionalism—this is not up to our standards. We will address this issue immediately to ensure all team members follow strict hygiene protocols and provide a comfortable dining environment.

review: A \"5\" for the Dosa, Lassi's, and the service!  On a separate occasion we took in-laws (from India) for the lunch buffet; they said it was the best they've had in a long time.  I would love to see them offer a Chinese Dosa or Genie Dosa!

review:
A few people have told me that La Paz just isn't the same since their move. Back in the day, they were a frequent lunch place of mine when they were on South Blvd.  I wasn't sure what to expect, but went with an open mind.

On my first visit I had the fish tacos - well one fish and one shrimp.  They were very accommodating in allowing me to try one of each.  They were good.  I would order them again.

The five stars come from my second visit.  I had the shrimp and steak salad.  They cook the steak perfectly to medium, as requested and the salad is simply phenomenal!   Their sun-dried tomato vinaigrette is delicious and was the perfect match.  If you have to eat a healthy salad, this is the one to pick.  The meat is spicy, tender and char-grilled.  

If you go to La Paz with expectations of their former self you might be disappointed.  I think they did a great job.  New look, new menu....just the name remains.  Plus, their house wine is one of my favorites.  How can that be bad?
key_point: The reviewer was very pleased with the food quality, especially the shrimp and steak salad, and appreciates the new menu and atmosphere.
business_response: Thank you so much for your thoughtful review and for giving our new location and menu a try! We're delighted to hear you enjoyed the shrimp and steak salad and our house wine. We appreciate your support and look forward to serving you again soon!
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review's main sentiments, and the business response is polite, relevant, and professionally appropriate.

review: I would give it a zero if I could brings a very unsightly crowd to a good area.

review:
So I will start off by saying about a month ago I was yelling to the TOP of my lungs everyone needs to visit the Fremont Casino its my favorite place!!!  They have the best tables!

Sadly I had a horrible experience and it has permanently changed my views of this place. I was playing at the Craps table ($3) and this very loud group next to me was playing and the man next to me on multiple occasion kept thinking my money was his. So in my mind I am like why aren't these people doing anything to prevent this? So it got so bad that they actually had to go look at the tape.

So here is my real issue. The man argued to the staff that it was his money on the table and I was nicely sad that it was mine but the game in no way could be stop while the conflict got resolved. So Money that no one could confirm sat on the table when all I wanted to was grab my money and leave. The Manager on duty Monica  was so rude to me she did look at the tape (for 10-15 mins) and saw that it was my money and when I asked for my $3 back YES $3 that they couldn't confirm that was mine she tried to tell me I was trying to get over and scamming them. I WAS LIKE ARE YOU SERIOUS you guys make $3 in 0.02 seconds and you can't afford to give me $3???? She told me I had to make an appointment to speak to someone higher than her. the customer service was TERRRRIBLE and it did not stop there.

The next morning I called and asked to speak to the Operations Manager and he got on the phone and told me he would take a look at it and give me a call back and pretty much said what do you want and we cant give you anything more than $3. I NEVER HEARD FROM THIS MAN EVER AGAIN! 

and I made it clear if you would've given me the stinking $3 I would've given it right back to you at the craps table. 


Just be careful at this place... I am so sad that this happened to me at my FAVORITE place.
key_point: The customer had a negative experience with staff handling a dispute over money at the craps table and felt the manager was rude and unhelpful.
business_response: We're very sorry to hear about your recent experience and the way your situation was handled. We strive to provide excellent service to all our guests, and it's clear we fell short in this instance. Please contact us directly so we can address your concerns and work to restore your trust in our establishment.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes the review's main complaint about staff and the manager, and the business_response is polite, relevant, and invites further contact to resolve the issue.

review:
The food here was pretty good, though we made the mistake of arriving about 10-20 minutes before close. We wanted to sit inside because it looked cozier than the tables in the middle of the walkway, but we were told actually they were starting to clean up in there so we would have to sit outside. No problem!

However, we felt immediately like the few waitstaff were visibly upset we had been seated, particularly our waiter. He looked tired, and annoyed he had a table. So much so we literally asked him 3-4 times if it was really OK that we were sitting down to eat. He assured us it was OK if we put the order in quickly. 

Overall, they pushed the food out quickly, and we all enjoyed our pizza/pasta. For the price, this place is quite the deal on the strip. You get a lot, it's in a nice indoor location, and the food was very good. If this was a normal restaurant I would give it 3 stars (entirely because of the feeling like we were a burden, the food was great), but the value vs others around make it a 4 for me.
key_point: The food and value were excellent, but the service felt rushed and unwelcoming due to arriving near closing time.
business_response: Thank you for your honest feedback and for choosing to dine with us. We're glad you enjoyed the food and value, but we're sorry to hear that our service felt rushed during your visit. We appreciate your understanding about our closing procedures and will work with our team to ensure all guests feel welcome, even near closing time.
judge_reason: The key point accurately summarizes the review, and the business response is polite, relevant, and addresses the customer's concerns professionally.

review:
I am so torn on this review...so buyer beware.  The real reason I am giving this place 4 stars is for the hot homemade donuts that they make for desert.  There are also 2 dipping sauces too...a chocolate and a buttery carmel...I love the carmel it takes the donut to another level.

Also during dinner they put fresh homemade dough pretzels on the table with a butter mustard dipping sauce.  These are really good and a different twist on just throwing bread on someone's table...plus it is the only thing you can get for free at the Wynn.  I feel like a winner!

We went with the pre-fixed menu and it was a good deal...you could choose a starter, entree and desert for $39.  I went with the rib-eye medium rare...it was suppose to be a peppercorn rub but I was able to get it without it...thank you chef.  The steak was cooked perfectly and came with Mr. Wynn's Special Steak Sauce...wow was it good...I had to ask for more because everyone at my table was dipping all of there food in it.

Personally the staff is okay here...I would expect to be more impressed with this being a Wynn Property but it seems like the staff here are lacking in training.  I have never seen this place packed in all of the times we have eaten here, so maybe the really good waiters do not want to work here.

My buddy ordered a calamari starter and it was so fishy I would have thrown it back into the sea.  The waiter asked if it was good and he said yes...but I would have sent it back.  If I had ordered that this place would be getting one star.

The Mac and Cheese Bites Starter was also amazing too.  But Mac and Cheese Deep Fried better be good.

I also started with the Lettuce Wedge with Blue Cheese and Bacon.  I had no idea that this could be bad.  First there is only one strip of bacon that was cooked two days ago sitting on top.  Maybe the Chef could have diced this up for me.  There was nice chunks of blue cheese but hardly any dressing...so it was very dry.

So as you can see I am giving this place 4 stars but that is for those amazing donuts...everything else is just really good or really bad.  I should just commit to the 3 stars but I can't do it.  I am just not a 3 star guy.

On Yelp it says that this place is Italian...there is nothing Italian about this place...there is one pasta dish but nothing else.  In fact, the menu covers ever food choice and is probably more American, then anything else.

There is also a full bar too, so drink up my friends it is VEGAS!
key_point: The reviewer loved the homemade donuts, pretzels, and steak, but felt the staff could be better trained and found the calamari too fishy.
business_response: Thank you for your detailed feedback and for highlighting our homemade desserts and steak. We appreciate your honest comments about our staff and the calamari, and will use your insights to improve our service and menu. We hope to welcome you back soon for an even better experience.
judge_reason: The key_point invents criticisms about staff and calamari not present in the review, making it inaccurate, while the business_response is polite but addresses issues not mentioned by the customer.

review: I went here twice for the time I have been in Las Vegas.  The first time, it was pretty good, Service was nice and fast, the food came out at a good pace, everything tasted pretty good.  The second however, I was not a fan at all.  There was one other table that was seated so I assumed things would come out at a steady time.  Boy was I wrong.  On my second visit everything was the complete opposite, and I wanted a nice bowl of ramen.  Please do not get it here, it is actually the instant ramen stuff, which made me so sad.  I feel like i should go in one more time to see how it is, but after the last visit I dont plan to anytime soon.
key_point: The reviewer had a good first visit but was disappointed on their second visit due to slow service and low-quality ramen.
business_response: Thank you for sharing your honest feedback and for giving us two tries during your time in Las Vegas. We're sorry to hear that your second visit did not meet your expectations, especially regarding the ramen and service. We appreciate your comments and will use them to improve; we hope you'll consider giving us another chance in the future.
judge_reason: The key_point accurately summarizes both the positive first visit and the disappointing second visit, and the business_response is polite, acknowledges the issues, and invites the customer back.
